laws.title: Pennsylvania Minimum Wage — Federal Rate ($7.25/hr)
laws.summary: Pennsylvania's minimum wage remains at $7.25/hour (the federal minimum). Philadelphia has a higher minimum wage for tipped workers. Pennsylvania has been attempting to increase its minimum wage legislatively for several years without success. Employers should monitor closely as legislation is regularly introduced.
compliance_actions: Comply with federal minimum wage ($7.25/hr) for Pennsylvania employees
Philadelphia-specific: comply with Philadelphia minimum wage ordinances if you have employees there
Monitor PA legislature for minimum wage increases

laws.title: Pennsylvania Overtime Exemption Salary Threshold — $875/week
laws.summary: Pennsylvania's overtime exemption salary threshold is $875/week ($45,500/year) — higher than the federal $684/week. Employees in Pennsylvania who earn less than $875/week cannot be classified as exempt from overtime regardless of their duties. This is the state floor for executive, administrative, and professional exemptions.
compliance_actions: Ensure all exempt employees in Pennsylvania earn at least $875/week ($45,500/year)
Employees earning below this threshold must be treated as non-exempt and paid overtime
Review your exempt employee classifications in Pennsylvania anunually
